name: Green Apple Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean sterilized jelly bag and cheesecloth. Do not peel or core the fruit; just wash and cut into pieces. The seeds and skin contain most of the pectin, so they should be cooked along with the pulp to assure a firm jelly.
2. Cover the fruit with water, adding more if needed, then add lemon juice and boil until tender. Remove from heat and mash.
3. Strain the fruit through the jelly bag. Do not attempt to rush the juice by squeezing the bag or mashing the fruit down; otherwise, the jelly will be cloudy.
4. Let the fruit stand in the jelly bag for 4 to 6 hours, or overnight if space is available. The following morning, add sugar (1 cup) to the juice and bring to a boil until a candy thermometer reads 220 degrees F. Turn off the heat and skim the froth.
5. Fill and cap jars. Ensure your pan is large enough so that the jelly can raise up in a rolling boil; this boiling motion cannot be stirred down. If the jelly does not reach the 220-degree stage, it will not set.
